title: Lesson 234
transcript: We've been talking about how we protect the applications and the development of applications as we go through the development lifecycle and the system lifecycle. This section deals with how do we assess the effectiveness of the controls that we've designed and we are using in the applications itself. And this section goes by really quickly simply because we have an entire domain, domain number six, that talks about how we assess, for example, the security controls that are built into applications. And so as a review, what we've provided here is one slide that talks about the methods that we can use in order to assess the effectiveness of security and the controls that we have within applications. For example, we do a risk analysis process that is part of the risk management process. We ensure that we are logging security relevant events as part of what the application does and we monitor those on a regular basis, so logging and monitoring. We've got good internal and external controls. We involve security in the procurement process of applications. We get involved in certification and accreditation that we talked about before, comprehensive technical analysis of a solution, in this case software. and how we implement that is the accreditation or management decision. We get involved in testing and verification and also what's called code signing which is what's using digital signatures to hold changes that developers may have made to applications, we would require them to digitally sign anything that they've made as a change to an application so that they are held to that because of the digital signature providing non-repudiation services.
